page: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory bandwidth and specific computational tasks. Large-block memory bandwidth tests show strong results, reaching 210.27 GB/sec for read-only and 92.5 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both register in the strong tier, with the latter achieving 196.62 GB/sec. In synthetic CPU testing, the Passmark physics test demonstrates strong performance, while the overall Passmark CPU score and database operations fall within the average range. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 364.66 tokens/sec on small 135M models, but performance drops to the weak tier when executing text generation on large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ns_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require high network throughput and premium external storage integration without the overhead of local NVMe drives. The primary tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage options. Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio, strong memory bandwidth, and average database operation performance, this server is suited for static web serving, intermediate-scale database hosting, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnv6 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz, supported by 128.0 GB of RAM. The architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions. No local storage or GPUs are included, though the instance is premium storage capable. Benchmarks show strong large-block memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, text generation performance for large 70B models is weak. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and network-heavy applications requiring external premium storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and premium storage capabilities, suited for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory allocation for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
No bundled local storage; premium storage capable
Strong large-block memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput
Top-tier text generation for small 135M language models
Suited for general-purpose applications and network-heavy workloads
